commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f93e171f0958e6345ed35301325fe9b4364dfca7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR refactors main-session restart recovery to add a lock-file fallback for running sessions without `abortedLastRun`, adds focused tests, and carries a whitespace-only plugin-sdk touch.

PR surface: Source +55, Tests +80. Total +135 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main only recovers running sessions with `abortedLastRun === true`, so a running session with an intact transcript and no marker is skipped. I did not run tests because this review was explicitly read-only.
commands.review_followup: Author or maintainer handling is needed because the code blocker is concrete but external real behavior proof is missing, so ClawSweeper should not queue an automated repair marker yet.

target.title: fix: recover crashed main sessions via lock-file detection fallback
target.body:
## Summary

After an unclean gateway crash, `markRestartAbortedMainSessionsFromLocks()`
may not set `abortedLastRun` because `cleanStaleLockFiles()` finds no stale
locks to clean. This leaves all `status: "running"` sessions unrecovered
when the gateway restarts — the user gets a brand new session with no history.

This adds a crash-recovery fallback in `recoverStore()`: when no sessions in
the store have the `abortedLastRun` marker, scan running sessions for existing
transcript lock files. A lock file still present at startup proves the session
was interrupted mid-run by process death.

## What changed

- Extracted `recoverSessionEntry()` to reuse recovery logic
- Added `sessionHasExistingLockFile()` to check for leftover transcript locks
- Modified `recoverStore()` to run a crash fallback when no `abortedLastRun`
  markers exist: recover any `status: "running"` session whose lock file still
  exists on disk

## Safety

- The fallback only activates when **zero** sessions in the store have
  `abortedLastRun`, so the existing clean-restart and lock-detection
  paths are untouched.
- A lock file at startup is a narrow signal: on a fresh gateway, no embedded
  runs are active, so no lock files should exist for healthy sessions.
- Sessions without a lock file are skipped — idle sessions are never falsely
  recovered.

## Verification

```
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 npx vitest run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts
```
- `Tests  40 passed (40)` — all 17 original + 3 new tests pass
- New tests: crash recovery with lock file ✓, skip without lock file ✓,
  selective recovery across multiple sessions ✓

Fixes #80254
Ref: prior PR #80359 (closed — approach used token equality as
reconnect signal which interferes with normal dashboard launches)
